a slide on useful Positron shortcuts:
    - run code
    - highlight text
    - comment code
    - open close explorer/viewer panels
    - copy / paste
    - restart R
    - insert code cell

packages notation: package::function

how to look up how to use a function:
    - Positron auto preview, shows which argument you are currently at
    - help(function)
    - ?function()
    - Search the web for official documentation, many (especially tidyverse) functions have extensive help pages with documentation and examples

Pipes work for all functions which take the data/input as their first argument (all tidyverse functions and many others, but be prepared to encounter cases that won't work)

tidyverse follow up:

a note on summarise():
    - since the tidyverse function work with tibbles (dataframes)
    - but many base R summary statistics function (mean(), median(), quantile(), min(), max() etc.) expect vectors, you cannot "pipe" a dataframe into these summary functions
    - hence the summarise function, it works by piping in a tibble/dataframe and outputs a tibble

wide vs long (tidy) data:
    pivot_wider and pivot_longer animations

as tibbles by default print only 10 rows, you can steer that manually using print, e.g. data |> print(n=25) prints the first 25 rows

joining data:
    - leftjoin etc

Missing values:
    - in R, NA is the missing value coding
    - is.na() returns True if an element/value is NA, false otherwise
    - e.g. data |> filter(is.na(variable)) returns only observations (rows) for which variable is NA
    - if you load in data, make sure to correctly code missings (many datasets use -99 or other special codes to denote missing values)
    - whole literature no imputation of missing values, but be careful. If you "impute" mean values into missing observations, what assumption does this imply?
    - Missing values are part of (real) data, there is no way of getting around this. Be transparent!

janitor, tabyl, kable

inline code example

ggplot continuation: main aesthetics (x, y, color, fill, alpha, size)
main geoms
scales, captions, labels
theme

introduce .gitignore file
